qa_pairs.question: How do you create a new user account on an Ubuntu virtual machine (VM)?
qa_pairs.answer: To create a new user account on an Ubuntu VM, use the command `sudo adduser --disabled-password USERNAME`. Additionally, the new user will require a key pair, and their public key must be added to `/home/USERNAME/.ssh/authorized_keys` on the VM, ensuring correct permissions and ownership.

qa_pairs.question: How can administrative or root user privileges be granted to a new user on an Ubuntu virtual machine (VM)?
qa_pairs.answer: On an Ubuntu VM, administrative or root user privileges can be granted by using the command `sudo visudo -f /etc/sudoers.d/90-cloud-init-users`. This command opens an editor where you can add a line similar to `USERNAME ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL`.
